title: Project Overview & Build
description: Start here to understand what TEMO.AI is: a WPF/.NET 9 Windows desktop app that AI-generates websites and deploys them to Vercel. The README is only a title, so the MSBuild project file is the real map — it targets net9.0-windows, enables WPF, sets Program as the startup object, and pulls in WebView2 (embedded browser preview), SkiaSharp/Svg.Skia (image processing), and Velopack (auto-update/packaging). AppVersion.props holds the shared version consumed by the build.

title: Application Entry Point
description: Program.cs is the process entry point and runtime gatekeeper: it runs anti-tamper tool-detection guards, initializes the Velopack auto-update flow, ensures the Node.js runtime is installed (the generated sites are Astro projects that need Node), and then bootstraps the WPF App. App.xaml declares the application and merged theme dictionaries, while App.xaml.cs checks for updates on startup and shows the login window first.

title: Login & Authentication
description: Before the editor opens, the user must authenticate. LoginWindow.xaml.cs calls AuthApiService to log in against the backend (sending a hardware id from HwidService for licensing/identity), shows error banners, and opens MainWindow on success. LoginRememberService persists optional 'remember me' credentials to AppData, protecting the password with Windows DPAPI. This is your first look at how the app talks to external HTTP services.

title: The Main Window Shell
description: MainWindow is the heart of the UI, split across many partial classes. Start with the shell: MainWindow.xaml lays out the editor panels, preview WebView, toolbars, and flyouts; MainWindow.xaml.cs (high fan-in) holds construction, WebView setup, project loading, and shared helpers used by every partial; MainWindow.Panels.cs toggles the editor flyout panels. Understanding this shell makes the feature partials in later steps easy to follow.

title: Domain Models
description: These plain data structures form the shared vocabulary across services and UI. SectionDefinition and ComponentManifest describe the reusable site building blocks and their editable fields; LayoutComponent represents a placed instance in a composed layout; FieldDef defines an editable content field; ThemePalette (high fan-in) describes the color palette driving CSS generation; ImagePlanItem describes a planned image task. Learn this vocabulary before tracing the services that produce and consume it.

title: Persistence & I/O Foundations
description: Almost every service reads and writes the generated Astro project on disk, so these utilities are widely depended upon (note their high fan-in). ProjectPaths centralizes resolving src/public/index paths and project markers; Io provides resilient UTF-8 file reads/writes with retry and caching; JsonFile is the typed JSON read/write helper; LineCodec encodes the line-based content/CSS exchange format used between the editor and the AI; SettingsStore persists the OpenAI key and last project path.

title: Site Generation Orchestrator
description: SiteGenerator is the top-level conductor (the highest fan-out file in the codebase): it copies a template, composes layout and theme, generates content/CSS/images via OpenAI, and writes the finished project. It leans on OpenAiClient — the HTTP wrapper for chat and image endpoints with retry, moderation, and billing-error detection — which feeds token counts into UsageTracker to compute USD/THB cost. These three form the tightly-coupled core the rest of the pipeline plugs into.

title: Layout & Component Composition
description: Building on the domain models from Step 5, this subsystem assembles the actual site structure. LayoutComposer randomly selects shell/body section variants and copies components into the project; ComponentStore manages the catalog of reusable components and copies their Astro/CSS assets; SectionCatalog caches and queries those definitions; LayoutStore reads and rewrites pages/index.astro; ProjectComponentSync keeps a project's components aligned with its active layout.

title: AI Content & CSS Generation
description: This is where OpenAI produces the site's text and styling. AiPromptBuilder assembles content prompts by combining per-vertical templates with brand fields; ContentStore turns the layout into editable field definitions and writes content back into the project source; AiCssGenerator sends the current theme CSS plus a reference image to OpenAI and applies the returned CSS variable overrides; CssStore reads/writes the :root custom properties in the project's theme.css.

title: AI Image Generation Pipeline
description: The image pipeline runs after content. ImagePlanBuilder discovers which image tokens the project actually references and produces ImagePlanItems; ImagePromptCatalog builds localized (Thai) prompts and alt-text per image role using style, render, and palette; AiImageGenerator calls OpenAI with concurrency, moderation retries, and composition; GeneratedImageWriter persists results as WebP and registers them in ImagesStore, the manifest for the project's images.

title: Editor Feature Panels
description: With the services understood, see how MainWindow exposes them as editor panels. MainWindow.Layout.cs drives layout composition with undo; MainWindow.Content.cs builds per-field content editing and FAQ groups; MainWindow.Css.cs renders the CSS variable editor with color pickers; MainWindow.Images.cs manages image cards, drag-and-drop, and WebP conversion; MainWindow.Server.cs runs the local Astro dev server and wires its output into the preview WebView.

title: Generation UI & Shared Controls
description: This step shows the user-facing generation flow and the shared UI toolkit. GenDialog configures a generation request (content type, count, brand) and enqueues it; GenQueueWindow runs the job queue, rendering per-item stage progress and billing/error logs, backed by the observable GenQueueItem model. Helpers/Ui.cs is the most depended-upon UI file — a central library of WPF builders for brushes, inputs, buttons, cards, and image loading used by every dialog.

title: Vercel Deployment
description: The capstone: shipping the generated site live. MainWindow.Deploy.cs opens the Vercel deploy dialog, whose logic is coordinated by VercelDeployService — it loads teams/projects through VercelApiClient (the Vercel REST wrapper) and runs create/link/deploy through VercelCli (which drives the Vercel CLI subprocess and extracts deployment URLs). VercelDeployDialog presents the whole flow with busy-state handling and streamed logs.
